Guides

When to Use ChatGPT Custom GPTs VS. Projects

Compare Custom GPTs and Projects to choose the right option for streamlining and scaling your ChatGPT workflows.

View source →
If you've found yourself saving prompts to your notes app or re-explaining context to ChatGPT in every new chat, it might be time to level up. ChatGPT offers two powerful options to streamline workflows: Custom GPTs and Projects. They serve different purposes — and choosing the right one depends on what you're aiming to do.

When to Use ChatGPT Custom GPTs VS. Projects

What This Is

If you’ve ever found yourself saving prompts to your notes app or re-explaining context to ChatGPT in every new chat, it might be time to level up your approach. ChatGPT offers two powerful options to help you streamline your workflows: Custom GPTs and Projects. But they serve different purposes—and choosing the right one depends on what you’re aiming to do.

This resource explains the core differences between the two, including what each can (and can’t) do, and how to set up each type. It’s based on current ChatGPT features available to Plus users as of mid 2025. Free-tier users do not have access to either Projects or Custom GPT creation.

Prerequisite: You’ll need an active ChatGPT Plus subscription to use these features. Make sure you’re using a browser or app version that supports the latest UI for Projects and GPTs.

Use this resource to:

Decide whether a Custom GPT or Project is better for your use case

Understand what’s possible with each

Avoid redundant workflows and wasted time

How To Use It

To Set Up a Custom GPT:

Log into your ChatGPT account with a ChatGPT Plus subscription.

In Settings > Account, scroll to the bottom to create your Builder Profile (required before creating any Custom GPT).

Note: ChatGPT sometimes moves this option, so its location may vary slightly.

Expand the left sidebar and click GPTs (formerly labeled “Explore GPTs”).

In the top right, click My GPTs or use the Create + button next to it to start building your own.

Fill in the fields manually by choosing Configure instead of Create. The “Create” option lets you build by chatting with ChatGPT, but it often misses key setup details—so its recommended to use the “Configure” option for full control.

Tip: Open a second conversation with ChatGPT in a new tab to refine your instructions as you build.

Name & DescriptionThis is how you label your GPT inside your workspace—clear, functional, and for your eyes only (unless you choose to share it later). You can also add an icon to visually identify it—either by uploading your own or letting ChatGPT generate one for you. Think of this as naming and branding a tool you’ll reuse often.

InstructionsThis is the blueprint—like the paper instructions that come with a piece of furniture. Your GPT is designed to perform a specific task repeatedly, and the instructions tell it how to “assemble the couch” every time. Use this section to define its behavior, tone, and how it should handle any uploaded files or conversation starters. It’s the most important part of the setup.

Knowledge FilesYou can upload PDFs, text docs, spreadsheets, or slides to give your GPT reference material. To reduce confusion or misuse, clearly explain in your instructions what each file is for and how the GPT should use it. If you don’t, it might ignore or misinterpret the content.

Conversation StartersThese are clickable example prompts that help you (i.e., the user) initiate a chat with your new GPT. But for them to work as intended, your instructions should anticipate each one—so the GPT knows how to respond accurately and on-target.

Custom ActionsOptional, but powerful. These allow your GPT to call external APIs—like pulling live data or triggering services. Only configure this if you truly need it. For most GPTs, it’s not necessary—but it’s a great perk when your workflow calls for it.

Test your GPT using the built-in preview, and then publish it—either privately or publicly.

Use a Custom GPT when you want consistent behavior and memory across chats, especially for tasks that require structured outputs, instructions, or tools.

To Set Up a Project:

Log into ChatGPT with a Plus subscription.

Open the sidebar and click Projects. This opens a workspace that keeps all related chats, files, and instructions in one place. Think of a Project like a dedicated folder with context memory.

Click New Project to create your workspace. Give it a clear name that reflects the task, client, or objective you’re working on.

Add context, goals, and reference files. You can upload supporting documents—PDFs, spreadsheets, slides, et al. But the most important part is the instructions box. This is where you tell ChatGPT how to interpret your files, what the overall objective is, and how it should behave across all chats in this workspace. Just like with a Custom GPT, these instructions are the glue. They shape the assistant’s tone, priorities, and memory. Don’t skip them.

Start chatting inside the Project. Every conversation you have in that workspace will stay aware of the files and context you’ve defined. You don’t need to re-explain your goals every time—you set the starting line for every chat.

Use a Project when you need to:

Organize related chats, files, and instructions

Keep work evolving over time

Store and reuse attached files or goals

Custom GPTs are best for repeatable outputs. Projects are best for evolving workflows.

Comparison Table

FeatureExplanationGPTProjectCan use any modelChoose the best model available in your account tier✅✅Specify custom instructionsDefine how the assistant should act, respond, and behave✅✅References attached filesUse uploaded docs as part of the assistant’s responses✅✅Can be tagged in all chatsInstantly accessible from any new conversation✅❌Makes external API callsConnects to outside services and live data via API✅❌Organizes all its chatsGroups related chats in one place for easier continuity❌✅Can share with othersShare with teammates or publish for public use✅❌Utilize built-in chat toolsAccess goals, memory, and file tools within the workspace❌✅Learns from other chatsBuilds awareness across conversations in the same workspace❌✅Adheres to account settingsInherits your preferences and saved configurations❌✅

Use this guide to eliminate guesswork and build your ChatGPT systems the smart way—whether you’re building something to scale, or organizing a long-term workflow.

DISCLAIMER: This resource provides prompts, instructions, and content to help professionals use AI tools more effectively. Because AI-generated outputs can vary, it’s your responsibility to review and refine them for accuracy, relevance, and alignment with applicable laws, industry standards, and your specific business objectives.

Related Topics:

Personalize Your ChatGPT

Guides

YouTube Videos AI Recommends

Learn why YouTube is dominating AI search citations and how to create videos that AI is most likely to surface.

View source →
Google AI Overviews cite YouTube in 18–29% of results, and other LLMs (ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc.) hover around 16%. YouTube is now the #1 most-cited domain powering AI-generated answers. This matters because AI should be pulling your YouTube videos — not just your website or social profiles — into the answers buyers and sellers read.

YouTube Videos AI Recommends

YouTube is now the most-cited source in AI search.

Google AI Overviews cite YouTube in 18–29% of results, and other LLMs (ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc.) hover around 16%.

YouTube is now the #1 most-cited domain powering AI-generated answers.

This matters because AI is (or should be) pulling your YouTube videos — not just your website or social profiles — into the answers buyers and sellers read.

Why AI Pulls From YouTube

AI systems consistently surface videos that behave like helpful explanations. These videos are easy for LLMs to summarize and cite because the content is structured like a video blog.

Characteristics of YouTube Videos AI Loves:

Directly answer a specific question

Target a clear niche or audience

Use simple talking-head, tutorial, or walkthrough formats

Speak clearly about one topic per video

Sound like an answer a consumer would naturally ask about

If your video feels like the cleanest, clearest answer to a niche question, AI is more likely to cite it.

Why This Matters for Agents

AI search increasingly surfaces answers, not links.

When a consumer asks:

“How long does it take to sell a home in [CITY]?”

“Is it a good time to sell my house?”

“What does $1M buy in this market?”

AI often displays a summary that includes YouTube as the evidence behind its answer.

Instead of “ranking” in traditional SEO, you’re now trying to be the video AI chooses as its source of truth.

This creates a new visibility opportunity for agents who produce niche, question-based videos.

How to Approach YouTube for AI Search

Think Niche

Don’t answer “everyone.”

Answer one audience at a time.

Think Question-Based

Each video should solve one question, cleanly and clearly.

Think AI-First

Ask yourself:

“If a consumer typed this question into AI search, would my video be the one worth citing?”

If the answer is yes, you’re on the right track.

Video Ideas by Niche

Below are the optimized, niche-driven video topics aligned with what AI systems tend to surface.

Use, adapt, or record directly.

1.) High-end Luxury Home Sellers

Think high-end homeowners, premium property owners, and anyone evaluating the luxury segment of your market.

Video Ideas

Is Now a Good Time to Sell a Luxury Home in [CITY] in 2026?

How Much Is Your Luxury Home Worth in 2026?

The 5 Mistakes Luxury Home Sellers Make in [CITY]

2.) Relocation Buyers Moving to Town

Think out-of-area movers, job relocations, and households trying to understand your market from a distance.

Search Optimized Titles:

Moving to [CITY]? What Buyers Need to Know First

The Most Popular Neighborhoods in [CITY] for Out-of-State Buyers

What $1M, $2M, and $3M Buy You in [CITY]

3.) Local Homeowners Considering a Move

Think move-up sellers, early-stage planners, or “we might sell in the next year.”

Search Optimized Titles:

How to Price Your Home Correctly in [CITY] in 2026

How Long Will It Take to Sell Your Home in [CITY] Right Now?

Is It Better to Sell Now or Wait? A 2026 Breakdown for [CITY]

4.) Frustrated and On-Market Sellers

Low showings, stalled listings, or nearing the listing expiration date.

Search Optimized Titles:

Why Your Home Isn’t Selling — and What to Do Next

How to Relaunch a Listing the Right Way in [CITY]

What to Do When Your Listing Stalls: A Clear Plan for [CITY] Sellers

Your job isn’t to create more videos — it’s to create better answers.

Focus on:

One niche per video

One question per topic

Clear, conversational explanations

Timely topics buyers and sellers already search

Providing the best answer AI can find

Videos (1)

Videos

Run Search Ads in Google AI Overviews – Ai Marketing Academy

Run Google Search ads using broad match keywords that appear inside AI Overviews

View source →
Google is placing search ads directly inside AI Overviews and AI Mode — the AI-generated summaries that show up in roughly half of all Google searches. If you're already running search campaigns, this is a visibility opportunity you don't want to miss. If you're not running them yet, this is a strong reason to start.

Community

Real Estate Specialization Page Builder

Create optimized service pages for real estate agents focusing on local expertise and client needs.

Real Estate Specialization Page Builder
11

Create optimized service pages for real estate agents focusing on local expertise and client needs.

[redacted-email]
•
3/4/2026

Great! Let's tighten that up to make it more streamlined and focused. Here’s a refined version of your prompt:

✨ Your Optimized Prompt:

# UNIVERSAL REAL ESTATE SPECIALIZATION PAGE BUILDER

### (AEO + GEO + SEO VERSION | ADVISOR MODEL | DYNAMIC CTA)

You are an Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), Geographic Search Optimization (GEO), and SEO specialization page builder for real estate professionals. Your role is to guide a structured intake conversation and generate a high-intent specialization service page to effectively address queries like:

“Who is the best real estate agent to help with [situation] in [location]?”

The page must:

  • Position the real estate professional as a trusted local expert
  • Be quotable by AI systems
  • Be geographically specific
  • Optimize for high-intent search queries
  • Maintain educational, trustworthy language

---

# GLOBAL CONVERSATION RULES

  1. Ask ONE question at a time and await the user’s response.
  2. Provide examples only as guidance.
  3. Require specific, original input, asking for clarification if answers are vague.
  4. Do NOT generate the page until all intake steps are complete.
  5. Do NOT explain the framework unless requested.

---

# LANGUAGE FRAMEWORK (MANDATORY)

Each section must combine:

### Nouns

Real estate agent, REALTOR®, Broker, cities, property types, life events, transaction situations.

### Verbs

Buy, sell, relocate, price, market, negotiate.

### Modifiers

Experienced, local, strategic, education-first.

Avoid pronouns like "We help you." Instead, favor indexable phrasing.

Example:
- Use “An experienced Burlington REALTOR® guiding estate property sales” instead of “We help you sell your home.”

---

# INTAKE FLOW

Follow these steps in order.

---

## STEP 0. REALTOR IDENTITY

Ask:

  • Full name?
  • Brokerage or company?
  • Phone number for CTA?
  • Geographic market served?

Wait for user responses to proceed.

---

## STEP 1. CORE SPECIALIZATION

Ask:

  • What locations should this page focus on?
  • What specialization or niche is this page about?

Require a clear niche before moving forward.

Examples:
- Divorce home sales
- Relocation clients

---

## STEP 2. CLIENT TYPE

Ask:

  • Who is the exact client this page is designed for?

Encourage specificity such as:

  • Out-of-state heirs selling inherited property.

---

## STEP 3. SERVICE VERBS

Ask:

  • What actions does the real estate professional perform for this client type?

Require multiple verbs.

Examples:
- Coordinate inspections, prepare homes for market.

---

## STEP 4. PROPERTY + SITUATION NOUNS

Ask:

  • What common property types or situations exist in this specialization?

Examples:
- Single-family homes, inherited homes.

---

## STEP 5. SEARCH MODIFIERS

Ask:

  • What realistic descriptors might someone use when searching for help?

Examples:
- Experienced, trusted.

---

## STEP 6. DIFFERENTIATION

Ask individually:

  • What makes your approach unique?
  • What processes support your clients?

---

## STEP 7. CLIENT PAIN POINTS

Ask:

  • What challenges does this client type typically face?

Examples:
- Pricing confusion, remote selling challenges.

---

## STEP 8. CLIENT OUTCOMES

Ask:

  • After working with you, what changes for the client?

Require concrete outcomes.

---

## STEP 9. TONE

Ask the user to choose two or three tones:

Educational, empathetic, authoritative.

---

# PAGE GENERATION RULES

After the intake, generate a specialization page that:

  • Uses strong nouns instead of vague pronouns.
  • References the agent and brokerage naturally.
  • Maintains Fair Housing–safe language.

---

# REQUIRED PAGE STRUCTURE

  1. SEO-Focused Headline
  2. Opening Authority Paragraph
  3. Who This Service Is For
  4. Common Challenges
  5. How the Agent Helps
  6. Process or Strategy
  7. Local Market Expertise
  8. Why Work With [AGENT NAME]
  9. FAQ Section (8–10 Questions)
  10. Educational Call-to-Action Block

---

### CTA Structure

Begin with:

“If you’re navigating a [SPECIALIZATION],….”

Conclude with:

Text “[AUTO-GENERATED KEYWORD]” to [USER PHONE NUMBER].

---

### KEYWORD RULES

Derive from the niche using one clear uppercase word.

---

# META OUTPUTS (REQUIRED)

After the page content, generate:

### Meta Title

50–60 characters, including niche, location, and agent or brokerage.

### Meta Keywords

Short, comma-separated list including niche, REALTOR® phrasing.

### Meta Description

150–160 characters, clear and benefit-focused.

---

# FINAL OUTPUT ORDER

  1. Full specialization page copy
  2. Meta Title
  3. Meta Keywords
  4. Meta Description

Do not reference the intake process in the final output.

Why This Works: This version is more concise and focused while retaining all key components. Unnecessary repetition has been removed, and the language is more direct.

Best Used With: This optimized prompt works best with AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude.

💡 Tip: Maintain clarity in each question to ensure detailed responses that provide useful input for the final specialization page.

View Full Prompt

Makes consistent image across your daily blogs

I want to generate a 1080x1080 real estate blog cover image for Nano Banana.

First ask me the following questions one at a time:

What is the topic or title of your real estate article or post?

What city or market is this about?

What is the agent name or team name for branding?

What brand colors should be used?

What brand style best describes the business? (Examples: luxury, modern, classic, bold, minimal)

After I answer the questions, create a ready-to-copy prompt for Nano Banana.

The final output should include:

A reminder: Turn on “Thinking” mode in Nano Banana before generating the image.

Then generate the full Nano Banana prompt using these rules:

Image size

1080x1080 square blog cover image

Hero title

Convert the topic into a short 3–6 word headline

The title should be large bold text on the image

This should be the only text

Visual concept

Create imagery related to residential real estate such as homes, neighborhoods, pricing visuals, buyers/sellers, contracts, or market trends

Include a single strong focal point

Design style

Match the brand style and colors

Clean professional layout

Editorial real estate marketing style

Layout

Hero title placed in the upper portion of the image

Balanced, minimal composition

Design rules

Only the hero title

No paragraphs

Avoid clutter and cartoon graphics

The final design should look like a professional real estate blog cover created by a brokerage marketing team.

Output the final Nano Banana prompt ready to paste.

Weekly Real Estate Blog Topic Generator

Generate 25 unique, engaging blog topics for real estate weekly.

Weekly Real Estate Blog Topic Generator
4

Generate 25 unique, engaging blog topics for real estate weekly.

[redacted-email]
•
3/2/2026

Thanks for the specifics! Here’s what I have:

Focus on real estate or related information (home buying tips, market trends, neighborhood profiles).
Integrate seasonal or timely themes when relevant.
Limit each listicle or ranking to no more than 10 items.
Flexible format for the topics.

I'll create a comprehensive, optimized prompt that you can use to generate 25 unique blog topics per week.

✨ Your Optimized Prompt:

You are a creative content generator specifically for real estate. Your task is to generate 25 unique blog topics each week that focus on real estate or real estate-adjacent themes, without repeating previous topics. The topics should include:

  1. Listicles (e.g., "Top 10 Neighborhoods in Westerville for Families").
  2. Rankings (e.g., "5 Best Real Estate Investment Opportunities in Columbus").
  3. Versus comparisons (e.g., "Columbus vs. Westerville: Where Should You Buy Your Next Home?").
  4. Hyper-local information specific to real estate in Columbus and Westerville, Ohio.

Ensure each topic is engaging, relevant, and tailored for prospective home buyers or real estate enthusiasts. Aim for variety by incorporating seasonal themes or timely issues. Each blog topic should have no more than 10 items, encouraging depth in each listicle or ranking. Use a friendly yet professional tone suitable for a real estate audience.

The output should be structured in a clear list format, and avoid repetition of any topic previously generated.

Why This Works: This prompt is designed to generate a wide range of relevant and engaging blog topics tailored to your real estate focus, ensuring variety and specificity each week.

Best Used With: ChatGPT, Claude, or similar AI content generators.

💡 Tip: Customize the generated topics further by specifying local events or timely trends in real estate to keep the content fresh and relevant.

Feel free to copy and paste this prompt into your AI tool, and let me know if you need any adjustments or additional support!

Real Estate Monthly Marketing Report Template

Create a detailed HTML report on single-family home sales for buyers and agents.

Real Estate Monthly Marketing Report Template
3

Create a detailed HTML report on single-family home sales for buyers and agents.

Anonymous
•
3/2/2026

Great, thanks for the detailed responses! Here’s a refined summary of what I’ll include in the optimized prompt for generating your monthly marketing report:

Target Audience: Potential buyers, investors, and real estate agents.
Data Sources: Top 3 zip codes based on data from the MLS CSV file.
Age Breakdown: Ranges of 0-5 years, 6-10 years, 11-24 years, and 25 years or older.
Seller Credits: Average amount of seller credits.
Keywords for SEO: Focused on the specific city and target audience.
Trends for FAQs: Highlight zip codes with the most sales and pending homes, and the shortest Days on Market (DOM) at the bottom.
Visual Appeal: High contrast boxes for data sets for better visual engagement.
Executive Summary: A brief overview at the top of the report for the LLMS.

With this structured scope in mind, here's the optimized prompt to generate your report:

✨ Your Optimized Prompt:

You are a real estate data analyst tasked with creating a detailed monthly marketing report in HTML format focused on sold and pending single-family homes. The audience for this report includes potential buyers, investors, and real estate agents. The report must contain the following sections:

  1. Title: Include a compelling report title.
  2. Executive Summary: Provide a brief overview that highlights the key findings and trends that industry leaders want to see.
  1. Top 3 Zip Codes: Identify the top three zip codes for sold and pending homes based on data from the provided MLS CSV file.

4. Age Breakdown of Homes Sold: Create a breakdown of homes sold categorized by the year built with the following age ranges:
- 0-5 years
- 6-10 years
- 11-24 years
- 25 years or older

  1. New Construction Homes: Include information on new construction homes sold along with their median prices.
  1. Seller Credits: Provide information on the average amount of seller credits that were granted.
  1. Median Prices: Compare median prices for homes with private pools versus those without.
  1. Days on Market: Include insights on the average days on market for sold homes.

9. Trends FAQ Section:
- Highlight zip codes that are seeing the most sales and pending homes.
- Specify which zip codes have the shortest Days on Market (DOM).

  1. SEO Optimization: Integrate keywords relevant to this particular city and the target audience throughout the report for GEO optimization.

Additionally, ensure that the report is visually appealing with high contrast boxes around each data set for better readability. Use proper HTML tags to structure the content appropriately.

Please make sure to include CSS styles for visual enhancement and organize the content using sections, headers, and lists where appropriate.

Why This Works: This prompt thoroughly covers all necessary sections of the report, includes specific data structuring requests, and places emphasis on visual appeal. Each part of the report is clearly articulated, which ensures comprehensive information gathering and integration.

Best Used With: Models like ChatGPT and Claude, which can effectively format HTML and understand context for data presentation.

💡 Tip: When executing this prompt, ensure accurate and relevant data from the MLS CSV file is used for optimal results.

Feel free to copy and paste the prompt directly into your AI tool, and let me know if you have any further adjustments or additions!

View Full Prompt

Create an engaging newsletter summarizing local real estate trends and insights for agents.

3/29/2026

You are a skilled content creator tasked with developing a comprehensive newsletter for local real estate agents. The newsletter should focus on summarizing the latest real estate trends, highlighting key changes in the market, emerging patterns, and noteworthy statistics.

In addition to trend summaries, include links to the top five articles that provide valuable insights, best practices, and relevant news affecting the local real estate market. Each article should be accompanied by a brief description and its relevance to the local agents.

Ensure the newsletter is organized, visually appealing, and easy to navigate. Incorporate a professional tone that resonates with industry professionals while remaining engaging and informative. Target a monthly distribution schedule, and aim for a word count of approximately 800 to 1,200 words. Include a call-to-action encouraging agents to connect with each other, share their insights, and participate in discussions related to the trends highlighted in the newsletter.

CLAUDE TOOL | Listing Image Renamer — Setup & User Guide

This is a browser-based SEO image renaming tool built specifically for real estate agents. It takes your listing photos and automatically renames them with SEO, AEO, GEO, and AIO optimized filenames.

CLAUDE TOOL | Listing Image Renamer — Setup & User Guide
2

This is a browser-based SEO image renaming tool built specifically for real estate agents. It takes your listing photos and automatically renames them with SEO, AEO, GEO, and AIO optimized filenames.

[redacted-email]
•
3/27/2026

This is for every major platform — Website, MLS, WordPress, Google Business, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and YouTube — then packages everything into a ZIP file ready to upload. No software to install. No subscriptions. No cloud uploads. Your photos never leave your computer.

Platform Requirement This tool runs exclusively in Google Chrome. It will not work correctly in Safari, Firefox, or Edge.

How to Install Copy the full HTML code provided Open a plain text editor — TextEdit (Mac) or Notepad (Windows) Paste the code into the editor On Mac: go to Format → Make Plain Text before saving Save the file as image-renamer.html — make sure the extension is .html not .txt

Open Google Chrome, drag and drop the file into the browser window

How to Use Fill in your Agent Info at the top — first name, last name, brokerage, and market — this only needs to be done once

Enter the property address and city/neighborhood

Select which platforms you need filenames for

Toggle on the blog keyword option if this is for a blog post and paste your text

Option: Create the Tool Directly Inside Claude If you prefer not to deal with a text editor, you can create the downloadable HTML file directly inside your Claude account in seconds. How to Do It

Log into your Claude account at claude.ai Start a new chat

Type exactly this: "Please save this as a downloadable HTML file"

Paste the full HTML code immediately after that instruction

Claude will generate a download link — click it to save the file to your computer

Open the saved file in Google Chrome and you're ready to go

Upload up to 5 photos at a time

Click Generate Renamed Files

Review the filenames, then click the green Download button to save your ZIP file

Repeat for the next batch of photos

Troubleshooting Green download button does nothing → Make sure you are opening the file in Google Chrome, not Safari or any other browser

JSZip not loaded error → You need an active internet connection for the tool to load its ZIP library. Connect to WiFi and refresh

File opens as code/text instead of a tool → Your file was saved as .txt instead of .html. Re-save with the correct .html extension

Platform buttons won't click → Hard refresh the page with Command + Shift + R (Mac) or Ctrl + Shift + R (Windows)

ZIP file is corrupted → This happens if you are not using Chrome. Switch to Chrome and try again

Important Notes You only need to do this once — save the file somewhere easy to find like your Desktop or a dedicated folder

Once saved, you never need Claude to recreate it again — just open the file directly in Chrome each time

The tool itself does not require Claude to function — it runs entirely in your browser

If Claude gives you a preview of the tool instead of a download link, type: "Please provide this as a downloadable file, not a preview"

If the downloaded file opens as code instead of the tool, make sure it saved with the .html extension and open it in Chrome

Using the Tool Inside Claude vs. Via the HTML File Inside Claude (Preview Panel)

The tool will visually load and appear to work You can fill in fields, select platforms, and upload photos

The filenames will generate and display correctly However — the ZIP download will not work

Claude's sandbox environment blocks file downloads entirely This is a Claude security restriction that cannot be bypassed

Via the HTML File in Chrome (Correct Method)

Everything works exactly as intended Filenames generate correctly ZIP file downloads instantly with one click Photos are renamed with zero quality loss Files are organized by platform folder if multiple platforms are selected

The Simple Rule Claude is for building and previewing the tool. Chrome is for using the tool.

Never use this tool from inside Claude for actual work — always save the HTML file and open it in Chrome. Think of Claude as the place you got the tool, and Chrome as the place you use it. 🙌

COPY PASTE THE HTML CODE BELOW:

<!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Listing Image Renamer</title> <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jszip/3.10.1/jszip.min.js"></script> <style> @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cormorant+Garamond:[redacted-email];400;600&family=DM+Mono:[redacted-email];400&display=swap'); :root{--navy:#1a2332;--gold:#b8973a;--gold-light:#d4b05a;--cream:#f5f0e8;--cream-dark:#ede6d6;--muted:#6b7280;--border:#d4c9b0;--green:#2d6a4f;--red:#8b1a1a;} *{box-sizing:border-box;margin:0;padding:0} body{font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;background:var(--cream);color:var(--navy);min-height:100vh;padding:32px 24px} header{text-align:center;margin-bottom:32px;border-bottom:1px solid var(--border);padding-bottom:24px} header h1{font-family:'Cormorant Garamond',serif;font-size:2rem;font-weight:300;letter-spacing:.04em} header p{font-size:.72rem;color:var(--muted);margin-top:6px;letter-spacing:.08em;text-transform:uppercase} .card{background:white;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:4px;padding:24px;margin-bottom:20px} .card h2{font-family:'Cormorant Garamond',serif;font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:600;margin-bottom:16px;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px} .step{background:var(--gold);color:white;width:22px;height:22px;border-radius:50%;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:.65rem;flex-shrink:0} .lbl{display:block;font-size:.68rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.1em;color:var(--muted);margin-bottom:6px} input[type=text]{width:100%;padding:10px 12px;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:3px;font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:.78rem;background:var(--cream);color:var(--navy);margin-bottom:14px;outline:none;transition:border-color .2s} input[type=text]:focus{border-color:var(--gold)} .pgrid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:8px;margin-bottom:8px} .pc{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;padding:8px 10px;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:3px;cursor:pointer;background:var(--cream);user-select:none;transition:all .15s} .pc:hover{border-color:var(--gold);background:#faf7f0} .pc.on{border-color:var(--gold);background:#fdf8ec} .dot{width:14px;height:14px;border:1.5px solid var(--border);border-radius:3px;flex-shrink:0;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;pointer-events:none;transition:all .15s} .pc.on .dot{background:var(--gold);border-color:var(--gold)} .pc.on .dot::after{content:'✓';color:white;font-size:9px} .pc span{font-size:.72rem;pointer-events:none} .dropzone{border:2px dashed var(--border);border-radius:4px;padding:32px 20px;text-align:center;cursor:pointer;background:var(--cream);position:relative;transition:all .2s} .dropzone:hover,.dropzone.over{border-color:var(--gold);background:#fdf8ec} .dropzone input{position:absolute;inset:0;opacity:0;cursor:pointer;width:100%;height:100%} .dropzone .ico{font-size:2rem;display:block;margin-bottom:10px} .dropzone p{font-size:.75rem;color:var(--muted);line-height:1.6} .dropzone strong{color:var(--gold);font-weight:400} .flist{margin-top:14px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px} .fitem{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;padding:8px 12px;background:var(--cream);border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:3px;font-size:.7rem} .fitem .n{color:var(--muted);overflow:hidden;white-space:nowrap;text-overflow:ellipsis;max-width:75%} .fitem .s{color:var(--border);flex-shrink:0} .btn{width:100%;padding:14px;border:none;border-radius:3px;font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:.75rem;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;cursor:pointer;transition:all .2s;margin-top:8px} .btn-gold{background:var(--gold);color:white} .btn-gold:hover{background:var(--gold-light)} .btn:disabled{background:var(--border);cursor:not-allowed} .prog{height:3px;background:var(--cream-dark);border-radius:2px;margin:14px 0;overflow:hidden;display:none} .progfill{height:100%;background:var(--gold);width:0%;transition:width .3s} .results{display:none;margin-top:20px} .ritem{border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:4px;margin-bottom:12px;overflow:hidden} .rhead{background:var(--navy);color:var(--cream);padding:10px 14px;font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.08em;text-transform:uppercase} .rbody{padding:12px 14px;background:white} .prow{display:flex;align-items:flex-start;gap:10px;padding:6px 0;border-bottom:1px solid var(--cream-dark)} .prow:last-child{border-bottom:none} .plbl{font-size:.62rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.1em;color:var(--gold);min-width:100px;padding-top:2px;flex-shrink:0} .pfname{font-size:.7rem;word-break:break-all;line-height:1.4} .tog-row{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;margin-bottom:14px} .tog{position:relative;width:36px;height:20px;flex-shrink:0} .tog input{display:none} .tog-sl{position:absolute;inset:0;background:var(--border);border-radius:20px;cursor:pointer;transition:.2s} .tog-sl::before{content:'';position:absolute;width:14px;height:14px;background:white;border-radius:50%;top:3px;left:3px;transition:.2s} .tog input:checked+.tog-sl{background:var(--gold)} .tog input:checked+.tog-sl::before{transform:translateX(16px)} textarea{width:100%;padding:10px 12px;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:3px;font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:.72rem;background:var(--cream);resize:vertical;min-height:80px;outline:none;margin-bottom:14px} footer{text-align:center;margin-top:32px;font-size:.65rem;color:var(--border);letter-spacing:.06em} </style> </head> <body> <header> <h1>Listing Image Renamer</h1> <p id="hSub">Fill in your agent info below to get started</p> </header> <div class="card"> <h2><span class="step">⚙</span> Your Agent Info <span style="font-size:.65rem;color:var(--muted);font-weight:400;margin-left:6px">— fill in once</span></h2> <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:12px;margin-bottom:14px"> <div><span class="lbl">First Name *</span><input type="text" id="agFirst" placeholder="e.g. Melissa" oninput="updateHeader()" style="margin-bottom:0"/></div> <div><span class="lbl">Last Name *</span><input type="text" id="agLast" placeholder="e.g. Menard" oninput="updateHeader()" style="margin-bottom:0"/></div> </div> <span class="lbl">Brokerage *</span> <input type="text" id="broker" placeholder="e.g. Compass, Keller Williams, eXp Realty" oninput="updateHeader()"/> <span class="lbl">Market / Region (optional)</span> <input type="text" id="market" placeholder="e.g. Greater Los Angeles, San Diego" oninput="updateHeader()"/> </div> <div class="card"> <h2><span class="step">1</span> Property Info</h2> <span class="lbl">Property Address (optional)</span> <input type="text" id="address" placeholder="e.g. 11222 Dilling Street" /> <span class="lbl">City / Neighborhood *</span> <input type="text" id="city" placeholder="e.g. Studio City, Silver Lake, Brentwood" /> </div> <div class="card"> <h2><span class="step">2</span> Select Platforms</h2> <div class="pgrid" id="pgrid"> <div class="pc" data-v="website" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>Website</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="mls" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>MLS</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="wordpress" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>WordPress Blog</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="google" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>Google Business</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="facebook" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>Facebook</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="instagram" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>Instagram</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="linkedin" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>LinkedIn</span></div> <div class="pc" data-v="youtube" onclick="toggleP(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>YouTube</span></div> </div> <div class="pc" id="allBtn" onclick="toggleAll(this)"><div class="dot"></div><span>All Platforms</span></div> </div> <div class="card"> <h2><span class="step">3</span> Blog Keywords (Optional)</h2> <div class="tog-row"> <label class="tog"><input type="checkbox" id="blogOn" onchange="document.getElementById('blogBox').style.display=this.checked?'block':'none'"/><div class="tog-sl"></div></label> <span style="font-size:.72rem">Align filenames to blog post keywords</span> </div> <div id="blogBox" style="display:none"> <span class="lbl">Paste Blog Post Text</span> <textarea id="blogText" placeholder="Paste your blog post here..."></textarea> </div> </div> <div class="card"> <h2><span class="step">4</span> Upload Photos</h2> <div class="dropzone" id="dz"> <input type="file" id="fileIn" accept="image/*" multiple onchange="handleFiles(this.files)" /> <span class="ico">📷</span> <p><strong>Click to upload</strong> or drag & drop<br/>Up to 5 photos · JPG, PNG, WEBP</p> </div> <div class="flist" id="flist"></div> <div class="prog" id="prog"><div class="progfill" id="progfill"></div></div> <button class="btn btn-gold" id="genBtn" onclick="generate()" disabled>Generate Renamed Files</button> </div> <div class="results" id="results"> <div class="card"> <h2><span class="step">✓</span> Renamed Files</h2> <div id="rlist"></div> <div id="dlArea" style="margin-top:12px"></div> </div> </div> <footer>Fair Housing Compliant · No resolution loss · Files renamed only</footer> <script> const AGENT = 'melissa-menard'; const BROKER = 'compass'; function agentSlug(){const f=slug(document.getElementById('agFirst').value.trim());const l=slug(document.getElementById('agLast').value.trim());return f&&l?f+'-'+l:'agent-name';} function brokerSlug(){return slug(document.getElementById('broker').value.trim())||'brokerage';} function updateHeader(){ const f=document.getElementById('agFirst').value.trim(); const l=document.getElementById('agLast').value.trim(); const b=document.getElementById('broker').value.trim(); const m=document.getElementById('market').value.trim(); const parts=[f&&l?f+' '+l:null,b||null,m||null].filter(Boolean); document.getElementById('hSub').textContent=parts.length?parts.join(' · '):'Fill in your agent info below to get started'; } let files = [], processed = []; function slug(s){return s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9\s-]/g,'').replace(/\s+/g,'-').replace(/-+/g,'-').trim()} function citySlug(){return slug(document.getElementById('city').value.split(',')[0].trim())} function trunc(s,max=79){return s.length<=max?s:s.substring(0,max).replace(/-[^-]*$/,'')} function toggleP(el){el.classList.toggle('on')} function toggleAll(el){el.classList.toggle('on');const on=el.classList.contains('on');document.querySelectorAll('#pgrid .pc').forEach(p=>p.classList.toggle('on',on))} function platforms(){return[...document.querySelectorAll('#pgrid .pc.on')].map(p=>p.dataset.v)} function handleFiles(f){ files=[...f].slice(0,5); document.getElementById('flist').innerHTML=''; files.forEach(f=>{ const d=document.createElement('div');d.className='fitem'; d.innerHTML='<span class="n">'+f.name+'</span><span class="s">'+(f.size/1024).toFixed(0)+' KB</span>'; document.getElementById('flist').appendChild(d); }); document.getElementById('genBtn').disabled=files.length===0; } const dz=document.getElementById('dz'); dz.addEventListener('dragover',e=>{e.preventDefault();dz.classList.add('over')}); dz.addEventListener('dragleave',()=>dz.classList.remove('over')); dz.addEventListener('drop',e=>{e.preventDefault();dz.classList.remove('over');handleFiles(e.dataTransfer.files)}); function roomFrom(name,i){ const n=name.toLowerCase(); const map=[['kitchen','kitchen','quartz-countertop'],['bath','bathroom','marble-tile'],['bedroom','bedroom','hardwood-floors'],['primary','primary-suite','walk-in-closet'],['master','primary-suite','walk-in-closet'],['living','living-room','open-layout'],['dining','dining-room','hardwood-floors'],['office','home-office','built-in-shelving'],['garage','garage','epoxy-floor'],['pool','pool','stone-deck'],['patio','patio','covered-outdoor'],['yard','backyard','landscaped'],['entry','entryway','tile-floor'],['laundry','laundry-room','tile-floor'],['exterior','exterior','curb-appeal']]; for(const[k,r,f]of map)if(n.includes(k))return{r,f}; const def=[{r:'living-room',f:'open-layout'},{r:'kitchen',f:'quartz-countertop'},{r:'primary-suite',f:'hardwood-floors'},{r:'bathroom',f:'marble-tile'},{r:'exterior',f:'curb-appeal'}]; return def[i%def.length]; } function blogKeys(){ if(!document.getElementById('blogOn').checked)return[]; const text=document.getElementById('blogText').value;if(!text)return[]; const stop=new Set(['the','a','an','and','or','but','in','on','at','to','for','of','with','is','are','was','were','this','that','it','be','as','by','from','into','about','has','have','been','they','we','you','your','our','my','not','no','so','if','all','more','can','will','would','could','should','do','does','did']); const words=text.toLowerCase().match(/\b[a-z]{4,}\b/g)||[]; const freq={};words.forEach(w=>{if(!stop.has(w))freq[w]=(freq[w]||0)+1}); return Object.entries(freq).sort((a,b)=>b[1]-a[1]).slice(0,10).map(([w])=>slug(w)); } function buildNames(file,i,cs,plats,bkeys){ const{r,f}=roomFrom(file.name,i); const bk=bkeys.length?bkeys[i%bkeys.length]:null; const feat=bk?f+'-'+bk:f;const e='.jpg';const n={}; const ag=agentSlug();const br=brokerSlug(); if(plats.includes('website')) n['Website'] =trunc(r+'-'+feat+'-'+cs+'-'+ag)+e; if(plats.includes('mls')) n['MLS'] =trunc(r+'-'+f+'-'+cs+'-listing')+e; if(plats.includes('wordpress')) n['WordPress'] =trunc(cs+'-home-'+r+'-'+feat+'-'+ag)+e; if(plats.includes('google')) n['Google Business']=trunc(cs+'-real-estate-'+r+'-'+f+'-'+ag+'-'+br)+e; if(plats.includes('facebook')) n['Facebook'] =trunc(cs+'-home-'+r+'-for-sale-'+f+'-'+ag+'-realtor')+e; if(plats.includes('instagram')) n['Instagram'] =trunc(cs+'-luxury-'+r+'-'+f+'-'+ag)+e; if(plats.includes('linkedin')) n['LinkedIn'] =trunc(cs+'-listing-'+r+'-'+f+'-'+ag+'-'+br+'-top-agent')+e; if(plats.includes('youtube')) n['YouTube'] =trunc(cs+'-luxury-home-tour-'+r+'-'+f+'-'+ag+'-'+br)+e; return n; } function uint8ToBase64(uint8){ let bin='';const chunk=8192; for(let i=0;i<uint8.length;i+=chunk)bin+=String.fromCharCode.apply(null,uint8.subarray(i,i+chunk)); return btoa(bin); } async function generate(){ if(!document.getElementById('agFirst').value.trim()||!document.getElementById('agLast').value.trim()){alert('Please enter your first and last name.');return} if(!document.getElementById('broker').value.trim()){alert('Please enter your brokerage name.');return} const city=document.getElementById('city').value.trim(); if(!city){alert('Please enter a city or neighborhood.');return} const plats=platforms(); if(!plats.length){alert('Please select at least one platform.');return} if(!files.length){alert('Please upload at least one photo.');return} const cs=citySlug();const bkeys=blogKeys(); const btn=document.getElementById('genBtn'); btn.disabled=true;btn.textContent='Processing...'; const prog=document.getElementById('prog');const fill=document.getElementById('progfill'); prog.style.display='block';fill.style.width='0%'; processed=[]; document.getElementById('rlist').innerHTML=''; document.getElementById('dlArea').innerHTML=''; for(let i=0;i<files.length;i++){ fill.style.width=((i+1)/files.length*100)+'%'; const file=files[i]; const names=buildNames(file,i,cs,plats,bkeys); const buf=await file.arrayBuffer(); processed.push({buf,names,orig:file.name}); const d=document.createElement('div');d.className='ritem'; d.innerHTML='<div class="rhead">Photo '+(i+1)+' — '+file.name+'</div><div class="rbody">'+Object.entries(names).map(([p,n])=>'<div class="prow"><span class="plbl">'+p+'</span><span class="pfname">'+n+'</span></div>').join('')+'</div>'; document.getElementById('rlist').appendChild(d); } prog.style.display='none'; btn.disabled=false;btn.textContent='Generate Renamed Files'; document.getElementById('results').style.display='block'; document.getElementById('results').scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth'}); await buildZip(plats,cs); } async function buildZip(plats,cs){ const dlArea=document.getElementById('dlArea'); dlArea.innerHTML='<div style="font-size:.75rem;color:var(--muted);padding:8px 0">Building ZIP...</div>'; try{ if(typeof JSZip==='undefined')throw new Error('JSZip not loaded — check internet connection'); const zip=new JSZip(); for(const p of processed){ const data=new Uint8Array(p.buf); if(plats.length>1){ for(const[platform,fname]of Object.entries(p.names))zip.folder(platform.replace(/[\s/]/g,'_')).file(fname,data); }else{ zip.file(Object.values(p.names)[0],data); } } const blob=await zip.generateAsync({type:'blob',compression:'STORE'}); const filename=cs+'-listing-images-renamed.zip'; const url=URL.createObjectURL(blob); const a=document.createElement('a'); a.href=url; a.download=filename; a.style.cssText='display:block;width:100%;padding:14px;background:var(--green);color:white;text-align:center;text-decoration:none;border-radius:3px;font-family:\'DM Mono\',monospace;font-size:.75rem;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;margin-top:8px'; a.textContent='⬇ Download '+filename; dlArea.innerHTML=''; dlArea.appendChild(a); }catch(err){ dlArea.innerHTML='<div style="padding:12px;background:#fdf0f0;border:1px solid #f0c0c0;color:#8b1a1a;border-radius:3px;font-size:.75rem">Error: '+err.message+'</div>'; } } </script> </body> </html>

Create a Detailed Comparative Market Analysis Report

Generate a CMA report for sellers, analyzing market trends and comparable properties in a neighborhood.

Create a Detailed Comparative Market Analysis Report
1

Generate a CMA report for sellers, analyzing market trends and comparable properties in a neighborhood.

chris
•
3/26/2026

You are tasked with creating a comprehensive Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report for individual sellers focusing on a specific neighborhood.

Begin by gathering detailed information about the seller's home, including the size in square footage, architectural style, year built, exact address, and parking availability.

Collect community statistics relevant to the neighborhood, detailing the average days on market, as well as the average and median home prices. Include the number of recent sales, current listings, and the months of supply to provide a clear picture of the market dynamics.

Incorporate information on comparable properties that reflect similarities and differences with the subject property. Key factors to prioritize in the comparison include square footage and layout, any notable upgrades or renovations, location proximity to amenities, year built, as well as the number of bedrooms and bathrooms.

Format the findings in a detailed report that includes visual aids such as graphs and charts to clearly illustrate trends and comparisons, ensuring the presentation is both informative and accessible to the seller. Make sure to highlight reasons why the subject property may be considered superior or inferior compared to the identified comparable properties, providing a well-rounded analysis to assist the seller in making informed decisions.

Key Clauses in Saskatchewan Commercial Leases

Identify and analyze essential clauses in commercial leasing agreements for industrial workshops in Saskatchewan.

Key Clauses in Saskatchewan Commercial Leases
1

Identify and analyze essential clauses in commercial leasing agreements for industrial workshops in Saskatchewan.

Anonymous
•
3/8/2026

Great! Here’s a refined prompt that will help you identify all necessary clauses commonly included in commercial leasing contracts for industrial workshop buildings in Saskatchewan, along with analysis of a provided lease sample:

✨ Your Optimized Prompt:

You are a legal expert specializing in commercial leasing agreements in Saskatchewan, Canada, particularly for industrial workshop buildings. Your task is to identify all necessary clauses typically included in commercial leasing contracts. Please provide a comprehensive list of these clauses without any specific focus but ensuring they cover all potential situations relevant to property owners.

Additionally, analyze the provided lease sample:
1. Identify and extract key terms and clauses from the sample.
2. Compare these findings with the general list you have created to highlight any specific clauses unique to the sample or those that are particularly important in the context of Saskatchewan regulations.

Format your findings as a detailed numbered list that includes explanatory notes for each clause, ensuring clarity and relevance for property owners in this sector.

Why This Works: This prompt effectively combines the need for a general overview of necessary clauses with an analysis of a specific lease sample, ensuring thoroughness and relevance for property owners in Saskatchewan.

Best Used With: ChatGPT or Claude

💡 Tip: When uploading the lease sample, ensure it’s clear and legible for the best analysis results.

Feel free to copy and paste this prompt into your preferred AI tool!

Real Estate Pricing Strategy Analyzer

Generate a data-driven pricing strategy for a property based on MLS data and market trends.

Real Estate Pricing Strategy Analyzer
1

Generate a data-driven pricing strategy for a property based on MLS data and market trends.

tapn2re
•
3/6/2026

Thank you for your patience! I appreciate the clarification. Based on your goals for analyzing MLS data and creating a pricing strategy, let me summarize:

You want to provide MLS data points like recent sales and comparable properties.
You want to highlight the subject property’s specific characteristics.
The target audience for this pricing strategy is the seller.
You’d like the output in a structured format, possibly as a detailed report.

Now, I'll create an optimized prompt for you that incorporates these elements and asks necessary questions for further clarity.

✨ Your Optimized Prompt:

You are a real estate analyst tasked with creating a pricing strategy for a subject property based on MLS data. The pricing strategy should include a suggested price range to present to the seller, along with pros and cons for each pricing point based strictly on the provided data.

1. Gather the following essential data points:
- Recent sales of comparable properties in the area (at least 5-10).
- Current market trends impacting property values.
- Key characteristics of the subject property (e.g., location, size, condition, special features).

2. Ask User for Specifics:
- What is the address and description of the subject property?
- What are the key details of the comparable properties? (e.g., address, square footage, sale price, date of sale)
- Are there any unique features of the subject property that could influence its value?
- What is the current state of the local real estate market? (e.g., seller's market, buyer's market)
- What timeframe are you considering for this pricing strategy?

3. Output Requirements:
- Present the suggested price range clearly.
- Provide a detailed analysis of each pricing point, including pros and cons based on the data.
- Ensure no fabricated data is included; all analysis must be grounded in provided information.

4. Conclusion:
- Summarize the rationale behind the suggested pricing strategy and any recommendations for approaching the seller.

This prompt will analyze the provided MLS data thoroughly to ascertain a well-informed pricing strategy for your subject property.

Why This Works: This prompt clearly articulates the expectations, specifies the data needed for analysis, and guides the AI in producing a grounded and analytical output. It also ensures engagement by requesting necessary details from the user.

Best Used With: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or other AI models capable of understanding real estate analytics.

💡 Tip: Ensure you provide detailed and accurate MLS data for the best insights and pricing strategies!

Interactive Real Estate Market Dashboard

Create an online dashboard analyzing real estate data for buyers, sellers, and investors.

Interactive Real Estate Market Dashboard
0

Create an online dashboard analyzing real estate data for buyers, sellers, and investors.

Anonymous
•
5/3/2026

You are tasked with creating an interactive online dashboard that presents a comprehensive analysis of the real estate market based on a local MLS data set. The dashboard should be organized into two main sections: one for all zip codes in the dataset and another specifically for private pool homes.

For the all zip codes section, include the following metrics: zip code, number of pending sales, number of sold homes, year built, heated area, and sold price. The data should reflect the most recent report available.

In the private pool homes section, provide the following metrics: zip code, year built, heated square footage, days on market, and median sale price.

The intended audience for this dashboard includes buyers, sellers, investors, and individuals considering relocation. Ensure that the data visualizations and user interface are tailored to effectively communicate the insights needed for these varied stakeholders.

Engaging Content for Empty Nesters in Pinecrest

Create supportive social media posts for empty nesters considering downsizing, highlighting nostalgia and new lifestyle benefits.

Engaging Content for Empty Nesters in Pinecrest
0

Create supportive social media posts for empty nesters considering downsizing, highlighting nostalgia and new lifestyle benefits.

cypressbaysaf
•
4/30/2026

Create engaging social media content targeted at empty nesters over 55 in the Pinecrest and East Kendallareas who may be considering selling their family homes. This content should resonate with homeowners who have recently experienced children leaving home and are contemplating a move to a community that aligns better with their current lifestyle.

Focus on addressing the emotional attachments these homeowners may have to their family homes, acknowledging the nostalgia and memories while simultaneously highlighting the excitement and benefits of downsizing. Emphasize the joys of finding a new home that offers proximity to their children and the lifestyle activities they cherish, such as pickleball, mahjong, bunco clubs, art classes, and senior fitness classes.

Tailor the content for both Instagram and YouTube, utilizing short, impactful posts for Instagram that capture attention quickly, as well as longer-form videos on YouTube that provide in-depth discussions about the benefits of moving. Ensure the tone is supportive, encouraging, and relatable, making clear the advantages of embracing a new chapter while still cherishing the old.

Craft a compelling script that welcomes families moving to a new area with engaging storytelling and essential local highlights.

You are an expert scriptwriter for a BOFU video engine designed to create compelling video scripts that convert leads into clients. Your task is to develop a script for a video aimed at families relocating to a new area.

The script should adopt a casual and friendly tone, making potential clients feel welcome and at ease. It should include a mix of essential features that highlight the benefits of relocating to the area, such as local amenities, schools, community events, and personal testimonials from satisfied families.

In your script, focus on engaging storytelling elements that resonate with the needs and concerns of relocating families, ensuring that it drives home the advantages of making this new location their home. Your goal is to craft a narrative that not only informs but also inspires action, encouraging viewers to reach out for assistance with their relocation.

Please ensure the script is structured clearly, with an engaging opening, informative body content, and a compelling call to action at the end. The length should be suitable for a typical video duration of around 2-3 minutes.

Design Vibrant Thumbnails for Notebook LM

Create modern, eye-catching thumbnails reflecting 'brain color' aesthetics for Notebook LM, focusing on vibrant visuals.

Anonymous
•
4/27/2026

You are a graphic designer tasked with creating stunning thumbnails for a project centered around Notebook LM. The thumbnails should reflect a visually appealing aesthetic that resonates with the idea of "brain color," which can be interpreted as vibrant, intelligent, and thought-provoking visuals that stimulate curiosity.

Consider the following guidelines:

Design Style: Ensure that the thumbnails are modern and eye-catching, employing bold colors and engaging imagery that captures attention immediately.

Color Palette: Utilize a color palette inspired by the concept of brain colors—think lively and stimulating hues that provoke thought and creativity. Make sure the colors complement each other harmoniously.

Imagery: Incorporate elements that symbolize knowledge, learning, and exploration. This could include abstract representations of neurons, books, or other educational icons that connect with the theme of Notebook LM.

Text Elements: If including text, choose easy-to-read fonts that are modern and blend well with the overall design. Aim for impactful phrases or keywords that align with the content of Notebook LM.

Dimensions and Format: Create the thumbnails in standard dimensions suitable for digital platforms, ensuring they are optimized for clarity and visual appeal.

Deliver a series of thumbnail designs that meet these criteria, showcasing a variety of interpretations of the "brain color" theme while maintaining a consistent visual identity throughout the collection.

Your primary audience for these SOPs will be the full-time administrator, who manages day-to-day administrative tasks and oversees all aspects of listing management and transaction coordination.

The SOPs should comprehensively cover all areas related to listing management, providing step-by-step guides that detail the processes involved. You are to include all possible scenarios that may arise in the course of these operations, making certain that each procedure is thorough enough for the administrator to follow without ambiguity.

When creating these SOPs, ensure that they are structured logically, with clear instructions that facilitate easy understanding and execution. Include any necessary tools, templates, and resources that the administrator might need to implement these procedures effectively. Aim for a format that is accessible and user-friendly, promoting efficiency and consistency in the administration of real estate operations.

Real Estate Market Analysis and Forecast

Analyze MLS data to uncover real estate trends, market conditions, and provide actionable insights for buyers and sellers.

Real Estate Market Analysis and Forecast
0

Analyze MLS data to uncover real estate trends, market conditions, and provide actionable insights for buyers and sellers.

cypressbaysaf
•
4/23/2026

You are a data analyst specializing in real estate market trends. Your task is to analyze an MLS file containing information on closed sales, pending sales (including PS and AC), and active listings.

Begin by extracting and organizing the data into clear categories: closed sales, pending sales, and active listings. For each category, calculate the following metrics:

Total number of closed sales, pending sales, and active listings.

Price reductions for both closed sales and pending sales.

Average days on market for each category: closed sales, pending sales, and active listings.

Next, identify patterns and trends based on this data. Evaluate the market conditions to determine whether it is currently a seller's market or a buyer's market. Consider factors such as inventory levels, average sale prices, and the length of time listings remain on the market.

Additionally, provide a forecast of trends over the next few months, taking into account seasonal variations, economic factors, and historical data. Based on your analysis, offer tailored recommendations for both buyers and sellers in the area. Include actionable insights that reflect the current market dynamics to help your audience make informed decisions.

Ensure your report is clearly structured, using headings for each section and including visual aids such as charts or graphs where applicable to support your findings and recommendations. Present all information in a professional manner, suitable for stakeholders in the real estate industry.

View Full Prompt

Engaging Candidates: A Communication Strategy Plan

Develop a comprehensive communication plan to enhance candidate engagement and experience for a title company.

Engaging Candidates: A Communication Strategy Plan
0

Develop a comprehensive communication plan to enhance candidate engagement and experience for a title company.

[redacted-email]
•
4/16/2026

You are a communication strategist tasked with developing a comprehensive communication plan for a title company seeking to engage candidates interested in joining its team. The plan should focus on providing regular updates and personalized messages to enhance candidate experience and maintain interest throughout the recruitment process.

Start by defining the objectives of the communication plan. These should include increasing candidate engagement, providing timely updates on the recruitment process, and fostering a sense of connection with the company.

Next, outline the target audience, which consists of potential candidates, including those who have applied, reached out with inquiries, or expressed interest in future opportunities.

Detail the communication channels to be utilized in the plan. Consider options like email newsletters, social media updates, and company blog posts. Specify the frequency of communication, ensuring that candidates receive regular updates—such as at least bi-weekly.

Create personalized messaging strategies aimed at making candidates feel valued. This may involve segmenting the audience based on their stage in the application process and tailoring the messages accordingly.

Include key content themes for updates, such as company achievements, team highlights, and insights into the company culture.

Lastly, establish metrics for success. Outline how the effectiveness of the communication plan will be measured, focusing on candidate response rates, engagement levels, and general feedback from candidates about their experience with the communication.

As you develop this plan, ensure that all communications are professional, friendly, and reflect the company’s values. Aim to humanize the candidate experience, making candidates feel as though they are part of the company community even before they officially join.

Make sure to maintain a professional tone throughout the reports, incorporating relevant market insights and showcasing the competitive landscape of the neighborhood.

Transform Your Wix Blog with Stunning Design

Elevate your blog's visual appeal while preserving original content and links, tailored to your branding and SEO needs.

•
3/16/2026

Here’s the complete prompt with the updated color scheme specified as Navy, Gold, Gold Light, Cream, and Crimson accent:

✨ Your Optimized Prompt for Wix:

You are a graphic designer entrusted with designing the user’s blog for Wix without altering, modifying, or changing any of the original content, transforming it into a stunning, visually appealing format that impresses readers. Ensure that the branded blog is aligned properly and looks stunning.

1. Keep the blog full length as-is and preserve all existing links in the blog, keeping them intact and fully functional.
2. Start by prompting the user to upload their blog post in a format compatible with Wix (e.g., Markdown).
3. Do not ask for a picture.
4. Ask the user if they have a preferred length for the blog post or specific sections to maintain balance and readability.
5. Include the testimonial:
"Johnny Sarkis is an exceptional realtor!! He saved us tens of thousands of dollars while purchasing homes from new home builders. His negotiation skills are superb. We love him for his professionalism and expert advice on all matters related to real estate." Design around this concept to highlight the value of the realtor.
6. Ask the user to specify their branding elements, including:
- Color scheme: Navy (#0A2342), Gold (#C9922A), Gold Light (#F0C96B), Cream (#FAF7F2), and a Crimson accent.
- Tone: Encouraging, proactive, professional yet approachable.
- Visual style: Clean, modern, and professional real estate aesthetic.
- SEO elements: Inquire about target high-volume keywords and long-tail keywords for AEO (Algorithmic Engine Optimization) and GEO (Geographical Engine Optimization) that they want to include in the design.

[Reminder: Please prepare both high-volume terms and long-tail keywords to improve search visibility effectively when running this prompt.]

7. Specify styles for text elements:
- Body Text: Set to 0.875rem (14px) for comfortable long-form reading.
- Headers:
- H1 (Main title): 2.3rem (smaller).
- H2 (Section headers): 1.8rem (smaller).
- H3 (Sub-headers): 1.3rem (smaller).

  1. Backlinks: Ensure all backlinks go to external authoritative sources (such as government sites, financial institutions, and industry resources) rather than to your own blog pages. Add external links to all major keywords in the content, conducting thorough research on relevant authoritative sources for keywords to ensure backlinks enhance the credibility of your blog content.

9. Incorporate interactive buttons and sections such as:
- "Share on Social Media" with links to the user's social media profiles:
- Instagram: johnnysellinva
- X (Twitter): johnnysarkis
- LinkedIn: johnnysarkis
- Facebook: johnnysellinva
- "Contact Us" linking to ContactJohnny.com.
- Include the user’s email (mailto link): [redacted-email]
- Add the office number: 703-357-9200.
- Add the name of the company: Keller Williams Solutions.
- "Read More Articles" section, suggesting related blog posts linked to ContactJohnny.com/blog.
- Add a new CTA: "Free Home Valuation Checklist" linked to Free Home Valuation Checklist.
- Replace the link for the "Get Your Free Buyer's Guide" to link to Get Your Free Buyer's Guide.
- Ask if the user wants to include a testimonial section, which should be prominently featured to boost engagement and credibility.
- Include a prominent Call to Action (CTA) section encouraging readers to engage further, such as signing up for this newsletter or downloading related resources. Present these features in a visually captivating layout that is responsive and adaptable to various screen sizes.

10. Ensure the design includes accessibility features, such as:
- Alt text for images.
- Color contrast checks for readability.
- The AI will determine appropriate font sizes for headers, body text, and CTAs, ensuring that no font size is less than 12 points while optimizing readability and engagement.
- Use descriptive text for links to improve accessibility and SEO.

11. Design the header and footer to include:
- Black backgrounds with gold accents, ensuring consistency with the overall color scheme.

12. Provide guidance on content formatting:
- Structure headings, subheadings, and bullet points for easy readability.
- Emphasize proper spacing and alignment for a clean layout.

  1. Optimize all images for web performance to ensure faster loading times while maintaining quality.
  1. Include instructions for how users can easily update their content in the future without needing to redesign everything.
  1. Suggest incorporating tracking methods (like Google Analytics) to monitor engagement metrics on the blog to help the user understand their audience better.

16. Format the output in two ways:
- An HTML version suitable for Wix viewing, ensuring it integrates the user’s branding while maintaining readability and engagement.
- A downloadable PDF version that is visually consistent with the HTML design, allowing users to save and share their blog content easily.

  1. Ask for testimonials every time, creating a section specifically to boost credibility.
  1. Provide feedback once the transformation is complete, asking if any adjustments or additional features are required.

Be creative and adaptive, focusing on aesthetics while ensuring the original content remains unchanged. Ensure that all links in the uploaded blog content are preserved, kept intact and fully functional, and add external links to all major keywords in the content to enhance authority and relevance.

This version includes your updated brand colors. If there are any further changes or additions you need, just let me know!

Weekly Real Estate Newsletter for Rancho Cucamonga
0

Create an engaging newsletter with articles, listings, and visuals to boost real estate engagement.

[redacted-email]
•
3/4/2026

Thank you for the detailed responses! Based on your input, I’ll create an optimized prompt for an AI to generate your weekly email newsletter campaign for Constant Contact.

✨ Your Optimized Prompt:

You are an experienced email marketing specialist creating a weekly newsletter for a real estate professional focusing on the Rancho Cucamonga area. Your goal is to enhance engagement and effectiveness for a Constant Contact template newsletter that is visually appealing and easy to read in under 3 minutes.

  1. Subject Line: Craft an engaging subject line optimized for high open rates, ensuring it avoids spam filters.

2. Sections of the Newsletter:
- Engaging Article: Write a 150-200 word article each week about community events (like the Founders Day Parade, fireworks shows, or new restaurant openings) or relevant positive real estate news (such as mortgage interest rate cuts, positive sales data from Southern California, or tips for preparing a home for sale).

  • Instagram Reels Section: Include a section with a direct link toone of the recent Instagram Reels posted on @aaronstel. Write a brief summary of the video topic (50-70 words) to encourage clicks and follower growth.
  • New Listings Section: Provide two sections that showcase new real estate listings from Zillow or Redfin within Rancho Cucamonga, Fontana, and Upland, priced between $749,000 and $2,300,000. Each listing should feature:
  • 4 to 8 high-quality photos
  • Property address
  • Number of bedrooms and bathrooms
  • Interior square footage
  • A compelling headline followed by a summarized listing description (100-150 words).
  1. Call to Action: Conclude the newsletter with an engaging call to action: “Considering Selling your home? Contact me for a confidential market valuation.”
  1. Visual Elements: Ensure the newsletter is visually engaging with an appropriate layout for photos and text to facilitate quick reading.
  1. Publishing Deadline: The entire newsletter should be created, optimized, and ready to send out by Wednesday each week.

Why This Works: This prompt incorporates detailed requirements for content, tone, audience engagement strategies, and actionable steps for potential buyers and sellers, ensuring a comprehensive and engaging newsletter.

Best Used With: This prompt is designed for AI models that excel in content generation and email crafting, such as ChatGPT or Claude.

💡 Tip: Consider customizing the neighborhood highlights or listings based on local events or trending real estate topics to keep the content fresh and relevant each week!

My installed skill library, cross-referenced against every relevant AiM item. Flagship skills (marked ★) are deep-dived with architecture diagrams. Each card shows which AiM items the skill enhances or replaces — and AiM items that have a personal-skill enhancement carry a green badge at the top of their card.

Personal ★

Content Creation Engine 📊

Unified 6-phase real-estate content pipeline: source ingestion → BOFU query generation (230+ queries) → Apify-powered Reddit/web ideation → 5-criteria BOFU scoring → TOFU/MOFU/BOFU funnel tagging → multi-platform script writing with AEO/GEO requirements + ElevenLabs audio tags.

The flagship content engine. Replaces Pantana's BOFU Video Engine + BOFU Blogger + Cowork Avatar Agent with a single integrated pipeline that goes deeper on data sources (Apify Reddit scraping, browser deep-dives) and bakes Bay-Area-specific Fair Housing guardrails, GHL comment-keyword lead capture, and AB 1482 references into every output. Built around a market-config.md per Graeham's identity. Hands off topics to vaibhav-template / heygen-video for rendering and to cinematic-hooks for AI b-roll prompts.

How it works

Modular real estate content generation system for Graeham Watts. Turns a single topic into a funnel-tagged, multi-platform content package grounded in live Bay Area buyer and seller data.

When to use it

Bay Area / East Palo Alto real estate content creation engine for Graeham Watts (REALTOR, Intero Real Estate, DRE# 01466876).

How to activate it

Listing Remarks Writer 📊

MLS public remarks optimized for AI-powered home search (ChatGPT+Zillow, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews). Walks buyers through the property like a tour. Condition-adaptive (fixer / mid / move-in ready). Noun-dense AI-searchable copy.

How it works

Write MLS listing descriptions that surface in AI-powered home search. Buyers increasingly search through ChatGPT (with Zillow integration), Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and similar — and these platforms can ONLY read the public remarks text. They cannot interpret photos, 3D tours, video tours, or captions. Every feature you don't name in the remarks is invisible to AI search.

Two-stage b-roll pipeline on higgsfield.ai, driven via Claude in Chrome browser automation. **Nano Banana Pro** (default, photoreal) **or GPT Image 2** (when readable text inside the frame matters) generates a 4K start frame → **Seedance 2.0** (or Kling 3.0) animates it into a 5–15 second clip.

LLM Council 📊

Karpathy-style war room. Runs a decision through five independent advisor sub-agents (Contrarian + four others) who think from fundamentally different angles, has them peer-review each other anonymously, then a Chairman synthesizes the final verdict — agreement, conflict, recommendation. For high-stakes calls, not factual questions.

How it works

You ask one AI a question, you get one answer. That answer might be great. It might be mid. You have no way to tell because you only saw one perspective.

Shared References 📊

Single source of truth for brand identity (identity.json), branding tokens (branding.md), data contracts, integrations, publishing-via-composio rules, skill-deprecation protocol. Audit script (verify_brand_identity.py) runs as a pre-push tripwire blocking any output that contains the blocklisted DRE.

This IS the Brand Style Guide I proposed earlier — and it's more sophisticated than Pantana's pattern. Every skill that produces output reads from identity.json at runtime rather than hardcoding values. The blocklist enforcement catches the DRE leak that happened 10+ times historically. Pantana's Brand Style Guide skill is just a markdown file.

1. LLM Listing Engine — adapts Pantana's AI Recommendation Playbook. Off-site citation infrastructure for getting cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview. Citation audits, profile engineering for 12 sites AI cites, Best/Top/Vs proof page generator, coached comment scripts. ~8-10 hrs.

2. Schema Builder — auto-generates JSON-LD for RealEstateListing, FAQPage, VideoObject, Person+E-E-A-T, LocalBusiness, Article. Pairs with website-builder for ship-ready pages. ~3-4 hrs.
